title: Pharmacosycea adhatodifolia
taxonomy: Pharmacosycea adhatodifolia is a plant species in the family Celastraceae. It is a part of the order Celastrales. The family Celastraceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the staff vines or bittersweet. This family is native to the Americas, Asia, and parts of Europe. The Celastrales order is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the Northern Hemisphere.

In some regions, Pharmacosycea adhatodifolia is used to treat various health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant's extracts are also used to make topical creams and ointments. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential side effects.
